concepts.finding: NGCF: collaborative filtering on user-item bipartite graph via GNN. Embedding propagation: user node aggregates features from connected item nodes, and vice versa, through multiple GCN layers. Captures high-order connectivity (3+ hops). Outperforms NCF by 8-15%. Key: explicit modeling of interaction graph structure matters.
concepts.implication: High-order keyword-intent connectivity via GNN. A keyword connects to an intent via: keyword-document-intent (2-hop) or keyword-keyword-intent (2-hop) or keyword-category-intent (2-hop). Higher hops (keyword1-keyword2-keyword3-intent) capture indirect relationships.
concepts.open_question: What is the optimal propagation depth (hop count) for keyword-intent graphs? Do higher-order connections improve or dilute intent signal?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: NGCF-style embedding propagation on keyword-intent bipartite graphs (2-3 layers) captures useful higher-order intent relationships beyond what direct neighbor models capture.
